jokes.text: I love it when you talk dirty to me. I cannot wait to see you tonight.
jokes.setup: Sounds like a business conversation about Q1 results and performance
jokes.punchline: Reveals it's actually romantic/sexual talk
jokes.explanation: Strong misdirection that recontextualizes the opening business talk as foreplay. Economical and well-structured.

jokes.text: Think slutty Grace Kelly.
jokes.setup: Asked what she'll wear to a museum gala
jokes.punchline: Oxymoronic description combining elegance with raunchiness
jokes.explanation: Perfect contradiction in terms that's highly quotable and captures the character's sophisticated yet sexual nature.

jokes.text: And the opposite of that just walked in.
jokes.setup: Just described visualizing slutty Grace Kelly
jokes.punchline: Liz enters, representing the complete opposite
jokes.explanation: Economical putdown that establishes character dynamic and contrasts with previous line perfectly.

jokes.text: Test, test, test. This is a test for Jack's card. ♪ Wonder woman ♪ Excuse me, how do you re-record on these things? Whatever. I'll definitely figure it out.
jokes.setup: Liz trying to record a birthday message on a card
jokes.punchline: Complete technical incompetence and random Wonder Woman singing
jokes.explanation: Character-specific tech failure with random musical outburst. Feels authentic to Liz but slightly drawn out.

jokes.text: You look like a prison weed dealer.
jokes.punchline: Highly specific and unusual comparison
jokes.explanation: Brilliantly specific insult that's both unexpected and weirdly accurate. Very quotable Jack line.

jokes.text: I'm coming back from a singles dodgeball match, okay?
jokes.punchline: Reveals pathetic dating activity
jokes.explanation: Perfect Liz Lemon activity that explains the outfit while being inherently sad and funny.

jokes.text: Oh, I forgot, only guys can get hurt there.
jokes.setup: Jack asks if she's wearing a cup
jokes.punchline: Sarcastic response about gendered sports protection
jokes.explanation: Decent sarcastic comeback but fairly obvious and not particularly clever.

jokes.text: As my good friend and fox-hunting partner Mary J. Blige would say, 'no more drama.'
jokes.punchline: Claiming Mary J. Blige as fox-hunting partner
jokes.explanation: Absurdist pairing of R&B star with upper-class British activity. Quintessential Jack Donaghy delusion.

jokes.text: Ruh-ro!
jokes.punchline: Scooby-Doo reference acknowledging the awkwardness
jokes.explanation: Simple reference joke that acknowledges the situation but doesn't add much original wit.

jokes.text: Eat it, bitch!
jokes.punchline: Aggressive trash talk at a singles event
jokes.explanation: Perfect character moment showing Liz's inability to read social situations and be appropriate.

jokes.text: His foot was over the line.
jokes.setup: Revealing how she 'talked' to the guy
jokes.punchline: She just yelled about rule violations instead of flirting
jokes.explanation: Perfect Liz moment - completely missing romantic cues and focusing on rules instead.

jokes.text: Or, as I like to call it, singles fart suppression.
jokes.setup: Describing wine and cheese tasting event
jokes.punchline: Crude but accurate description of the social dynamics
jokes.explanation: Gross but accurate observation that captures both the pretension and bodily reality of such events.

jokes.text: I look forward to not watching that on an airplane.
jokes.setup: Jenna mentions her upcoming movie role
jokes.punchline: Perfectly cutting dismissal of straight-to-video quality
jokes.explanation: Perfect putdown that's both economical and devastatingly accurate about airline movie quality.

jokes.text: Not now, Jackie D. I heard on the walkie-talkies that there's a redheaded MILF walking around with some executive.
jokes.setup: Jack trying to introduce Tracy to Nancy
jokes.punchline: Tracy is actually looking for Nancy but doesn't realize it
jokes.explanation: Good ironic moment where Tracy unknowingly describes the person standing right there.

jokes.text: You shut your mouth!
jokes.setup: Nancy asking if they've met before
jokes.punchline: Kenneth's aggressive overreaction to innocent question
jokes.explanation: Classic Kenneth weirdness - inexplicably hostile response to polite small talk.

jokes.text: The grown-up dating world is like your haircut. Sometimes awkward triangles occur.
jokes.punchline: Compares love triangles to Liz's bad haircut geometry
jokes.explanation: Clever metaphor that insults Liz while explaining his situation. Well-structured and quotable.

jokes.text: I feel like you've been saving that one.
jokes.punchline: Liz recognizes it's too polished to be spontaneous
jokes.explanation: Meta-joke about the writers' process, acknowledging when a line is too good to be improvised.

jokes.text: Why would a lady get divorced at 50? Stick it out! Men die first, then you have two wonderful years, then you die.
jokes.punchline: Cynical but statistically accurate relationship advice
jokes.explanation: Brilliant dark humor that's both funny and grimly accurate. Peak Liz Lemon cynicism delivered perfectly.

jokes.text: Mrs. Doubtfire shimself could not do this.
jokes.setup: Warning Jack he can't juggle two women
jokes.punchline: Malapropism combining 'himself' with gender-bending reference
jokes.explanation: Cute wordplay that feels authentically Liz-like in its awkward construction.

jokes.text: Oh, god! The band is soaking!
jokes.setup: Liz forces rec specs on Jack
jokes.punchline: Jack's horrified reaction to sweaty headband
jokes.explanation: Jack's germaphobe tendencies meet athletic equipment. Character-specific but simple physical comedy.

jokes.text: A guy on the subway just called me a biggledeeboo.
jokes.punchline: Made-up archaic racial slur
jokes.explanation: Absurd fake word that sounds plausibly historical while being completely ridiculous.

jokes.text: I've learned the word 'black' in every language, just so I'll know when to be offended. Russian, chernyi. Korean, hoog-een.
jokes.punchline: Paranoid preparation with fake Korean word
jokes.explanation: Absurd paranoia mixed with fake language knowledge. Perfect Tracy Jordan logic.

jokes.text: It's an 18th-century word for 'dark-skinned moor.'
jokes.punchline: Fake historical context that sounds scholarly
jokes.explanation: Good followup that maintains the absurdist premise with fake authority

jokes.text: Dolphin... Eeeeeeeeeeeee! Eeeee-eeeee-ee-eee-eee-eee!
jokes.punchline: Claims dolphins have a racial slur, makes dolphin sounds
jokes.explanation: Completely absurd extension of the premise that dolphins would have racist language.

jokes.text: Barry Obams is the one who brought it back!
jokes.punchline: Blames Obama while mispronouncing his name
jokes.explanation: Typical Tracy mispronunciation combined with backwards political logic.

jokes.text: Aah! Black man!
jokes.punchline: Explicitly racist commercial that proves his point
jokes.explanation: Validates Tracy's paranoid theory with absurdly blatant example.

jokes.text: All you've ever known is your affirmative-action job and queen latifah covergirl commercials.
jokes.setup: Talking to Toofer about changing times
jokes.punchline: Specific cultural references that date the conversation
jokes.explanation: Decent observation about cultural progress markers, but not particularly sharp.

jokes.text: I'm Jerem! I collect posters.
jokes.punchline: Pathetic self-introduction with sad hobby
jokes.explanation: Efficiently establishes pathetic character with minimal dialogue.

jokes.text: I didn't have the heart to tell Mrs. Schwitzer that you've moved to New York, so I told her you were in jail for manslaughter.
jokes.punchline: Casual revelation of elaborate lie to candy shop owner
jokes.explanation: Perfectly casual delivery of shocking information. Great character moment showing Nancy's twisted kindness.

jokes.text: Peanut butter and Miller high life.
jokes.punchline: Disgusting combination of beer and peanut butter
jokes.explanation: Gross flavor combination that reveals their shared bad taste from youth.

jokes.text: You're a wackadoo, Donaghy.
jokes.setup: Jack giving non-answer about which relationship is better
jokes.punchline: Childish insult that contrasts with her sophistication
jokes.explanation: Simple but character-revealing word choice that shows their casual dynamic.

jokes.text: You always know you're at the right party when it feels like the Riddler is about to attack.
jokes.punchline: Batman villain reference for fancy party atmosphere
jokes.explanation: Brilliant observation linking upscale party aesthetic to Batman villain lairs.

jokes.text: Money bunny Avery Jessup steps out with sexecutive Jack Donaghy.
jokes.punchline: Tabloid-style portmanteau words
jokes.explanation: Good wordplay that captures tabloid language and Avery's media savvy.

jokes.text: Like Santa claus taking a shower.
jokes.setup: Explaining why photo wouldn't look right
jokes.punchline: Bizarre analogy for inappropriate imagery
jokes.explanation: Completely unexpected analogy that somehow makes perfect sense for 'wrong but not illegal.'

jokes.text: That is a 15th-century term for a black pirate! Racist!
jokes.punchline: Toofer applies Tracy's logic about archaic racial terms
jokes.explanation: Good callback that extends Tracy's absurd premise through a different character.

jokes.text: Then why are my paychecks a different color than Frank's?
jokes.punchline: Literal different colored checks suggesting different budget source
jokes.explanation: Subtle workplace reality about diversity hires having different funding sources.

jokes.text: Look, Toof, you provide a point of view that is essential to keeping the diversity guy from bothering us.
jokes.setup: Trying to reassure Toofer about his value
jokes.punchline: Accidentally confirms he's there for tokenism
jokes.explanation: Perfect Liz moment - trying to help but making it worse with brutal honesty.

jokes.text: 'I'm cool'? No, I'm sorry. You can't set me up like that. Don't quit.
jokes.punchline: Liz subverts the setup with wrong guess then breaks fourth wall
jokes.explanation: Brilliant meta-joke where Liz recognizes and rejects the obvious comedy setup. Very clever writing.

jokes.text: And you look like you won second prize in a beauty contest... Collect $10.
jokes.punchline: Turns it into Monopoly Community Chest card reference
jokes.explanation: Clever Monopoly reference that maintains the game theme while being a backhanded compliment.

jokes.text: Left over from my amtrak ride.
jokes.punchline: She brought her own cheap train alcohol
jokes.explanation: Good character contrast - Nancy's practical frugality vs Jack's luxury world.

jokes.text: I got the night bloats.
jokes.setup: Explaining why to wait until morning for sex
jokes.punchline: Unromantic but honest physical reality
jokes.explanation: Refreshingly honest female character acknowledging unglamorous bodily functions.

jokes.text: I thought you said this wasn't going to be sexual.
jokes.setup: Lee Marvin marathon comes on TV
jokes.punchline: Implies Lee Marvin is arousing to Jack
jokes.explanation: Good character joke about Jack's hypermasculine movie preferences being homoerotic.

jokes.text: Here are the bullet points: Jeter's apartment. We're playing beer pong. It's New York Yankees versus former mayors. I got 10 grand on the mayors.
jokes.punchline: Ridiculous party scenario with impossible betting pool
jokes.explanation: Absurd high-society party scenario that epitomizes Jack's ridiculous social circle.

jokes.text: Like three's company, it's titillating, yet anxiety-producing.
jokes.punchline: Compares to classic sitcom with sexual tension
jokes.explanation: Perfect analogy that captures the sexual tension and misunderstandings of his situation.

jokes.text: Aw, man, being Janet sucks!
jokes.setup: Jack assigns her Janet role in Three's Company analogy
jokes.punchline: Liz complains about getting the straight woman role
jokes.explanation: Meta-joke about sitcom roles and Liz always being the sensible one.

jokes.text: How do you choose between Lee Marvin and Derek jeter?
jokes.punchline: Metaphorically comparing the two women to masculine icons
jokes.explanation: Perfect metaphor that captures the old vs new, rugged vs polished choice in masculine terms.

jokes.text: On looks, Lee Marvin.
jokes.setup: Jack asks how to choose between Lee Marvin and Derek Jeter
jokes.punchline: Deadpan response choosing the less conventionally attractive option
jokes.explanation: Perfect deadpan response that subverts expectations and reveals Liz's unconventional taste.

jokes.text: This is gender inequity out the Yang!
jokes.setup: Complaining about Jack's romantic success vs her struggles
jokes.punchline: Malapropism of 'out the wazoo' with 'yang'
jokes.explanation: Typical Liz malapropism but not particularly clever or memorable.

jokes.text: Meanwhile, I'm reading a book called hiding your arms, hiding your anger.
jokes.setup: Complaining about aging differently than men
jokes.punchline: Fake self-help book title about middle-aged women's issues
jokes.explanation: Brilliant fake book title that perfectly captures middle-aged women's beauty and emotional struggles.

jokes.text: And even worse, they're both going to lose me.
jokes.punchline: Narcissistically focuses on their loss rather than his
jokes.explanation: Perfect Jack narcissism - even his concern is ultimately about himself.

jokes.text: Oh, yeah? How come I'm always forced to play ridicarus characters that don't enununcia tewell.
jokes.punchline: Mispronounces words while complaining about playing inarticulate characters
jokes.explanation: Brilliant meta-joke where Tracy proves his own point by mispronouncing while complaining about it. Peak 30 Rock writing.

jokes.text: You know, there actually hasn't been a white Princess since 1991.
jokes.punchline: Deadpan statistical observation that subverts the complaint
jokes.explanation: Sharp observation that reveals the absurdity of white victimhood narratives.

jokes.text: It's a summer camp that teaches pretty blonde girls how to be mean.
jokes.punchline: Completely backwards charity that makes privileged kids worse
jokes.explanation: Perfect Jenna moment - narcissistically helping privileged kids become more like her awful self.

jokes.text: Or that you're my prostitute.
jokes.setup: Avery saying people might think Jack's her dad
jokes.punchline: Equally unflattering alternative interpretation
jokes.explanation: Sharp comeback that deflates Avery's concern with an equally awkward scenario.

jokes.text: The sportscaster who bites ladies.
jokes.setup: Jack asks if she knows Lee Marvin
jokes.punchline: Confuses Lee Marvin with Mike Tyson
jokes.explanation: Good generational gap joke showing Avery's cultural blind spots.

jokes.text: Don't ask how I got them. But I do know the access code to his pyramid.
jokes.punchline: Casual mention of stealing from grave plus pyramid conspiracy
jokes.explanation: Absurd escalation that combines grave robbing with conspiracy theories casually.

jokes.text: I found myself rooting for our boys in uniform, not those Blue hippies.
jokes.punchline: Completely misses the anti-war message
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing political obliviousness to obvious movie messaging.

jokes.text: Some of them were former marines, but they were mercenaries working for a space mining company.
jokes.punchline: Liz obsessively corrects fictional military details
jokes.explanation: Perfect Liz moment - prioritizing technical accuracy over social connection.

jokes.text: The last time I used a birth control device, it was the '70s, and it looked like soap on a rope.
jokes.punchline: Vivid description of outdated contraceptives
jokes.explanation: Funny historical reference with vivid imagery about outdated birth control methods.

jokes.text: I want him to genuinely like me, even when I'm old.
jokes.setup: Liz listing what she wants in a man
jokes.punchline: Surprisingly vulnerable and realistic relationship goal
jokes.explanation: Touching moment that grounds the comedy in real emotional honesty.

jokes.text: You making much English. This is fun alcoholics meeting!
jokes.punchline: Broken English reveals it's an AA meeting, not wine tasting
jokes.explanation: Great reveal that recontextualizes the entire wine and cheese scene as an AA meeting.

jokes.text: You were only hired by the second city because they needed someone who could remember to feed the theater cat!
jokes.punchline: She was hired for pet care, not comedy talent
jokes.explanation: Good deflation of Liz's comedy credentials with mundane but character-appropriate reason.

jokes.text: You attended the university of Maryland on a partial competitive jazz dance scholarship.
jokes.setup: Pete revealing Liz's affirmative action benefits
jokes.punchline: Specific embarrassing scholarship detail
jokes.explanation: Good revelation that fits Liz's character while advancing plot

jokes.text: Happy birthday, bitches!
jokes.setup: Reference to the show that was cancelled for TGS
jokes.punchline: Over-the-top masculine catchphrase
jokes.explanation: Simple callback that illustrates the absurdly macho show TGS replaced.

jokes.text: Why do you think your checks aren't the same color as Howie mandel's?
jokes.punchline: References the different colored paycheck joke
jokes.explanation: Decent callback but doesn't add much new to the established joke.

jokes.text: Okay, fine, fine! Toofer. I'll just go back to Toofer.
jokes.setup: Hearing all the worse nickname options
jokes.punchline: Toofer's defeated acceptance of original name
jokes.explanation: Perfect structure - Toofer realizes his original nickname was actually better

jokes.text: Three-fer, 'cause you're also gay.
jokes.punchline: Adding sexual orientation to the twofer concept
jokes.explanation: Logical extension of the twofer concept but fairly obvious and not particularly clever.
